case_name: ABB Holdings Pte Ltd and Others v Sher Hock Guan Charles
ABB Holdings Pte Ltd and Others v Sher Hock Guan Charles
party: ABB Installation Materials (East Asia) Pte Ltd

fact_changes.from: From January 1997 to January 2000, Sher Hock Guan Charles held the position of President of ABB Installation Materials (East Asia) Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.to: From January 1997 to January 2000, Sher Hock Guan Charles held the position of President of the second plaintiff.

fact_changes.from: Between January 1997 and January 2001, ABB Installation Materials (East Asia) Pte Ltd was the employing entity for Sher Hock Guan Charles under contracts issued by ABB Holdings Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.to: Between January 1997 and January 2001, the second plaintiff was the employing entity for Sher Hock Guan Charles.

fact_changes.from: On 1 January 2001, the employment of Sher Hock Guan Charles was transferred from ABB Installation Materials (East Asia) Pte Ltd to ABB Industry Pte Ltd pursuant to a new organisation structure for the ABB Singapore Group.
fact_changes.to: On 15 January 2001, pursuant to a new organisation structure for the ABB Singapore Group which had come into effect on 1 January 2001, his employment was transferred from the second plaintiff to the third plaintiff with retrospective effect to 1 January 2001.

fact_changes.from: The 19 July 1996 employment contract issued by ABB Holdings Pte Ltd stated that Sher Hock Guan Charles would serve as President of ABB Installation Materials (East Asia) Pte Ltd and that his appointment was the General Terms and Conditions of Employment of the Country Organisation.
fact_changes.to: The 19 July 1996 employment contract between the first plaintiff and Sher Hock Guan Charles stated that he would serve as President of the second plaintiff and use his best endeavours to promote the interest and welfare of the ABB Group.

fact_changes.from: The 20 September 1999 employment contract, effective 1 February 2000, identified ABB Installation Materials (East Asia) Pte Ltd as the employing entity for Sher Hock Guan Charles and included clauses on secrecy of office matters and a prohibition on private participation in other business activities, especially in the company’s line of business.
fact_changes.to: The 20 September 1999 employment contract, effective 1 February 2000, was between the first plaintiff and Sher Hock Guan Charles and included clauses requiring secrecy in office matters and prohibiting private participation in other business activities, especially in the line of business.

case_name: ABB Holdings Pte Ltd and Others v Sher Hock Guan Charles

fact_changes.from: ABB Industry Pte Ltd is a wholly owned subsidiary of ABB Holdings Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.to: ABB Holdings Pte Ltd was the first plaintiff and was the sole shareholder of ABB Industry Pte Ltd.

fact_changes.from: From 1 January 2001 to February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles held the position of General Manager of ABB Industry Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.to: From 1 January 2001 to February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles held the position of General Manager of the third plaintiff.

fact_changes.from: From September 2001 to February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles held the position of Vice-President and Head of Business Area Low Voltage Products, Automation Technology Products Division Asia South, in ABB Industry Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.to: From September 2001 to February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles held the position of Vice-President and Head of Business Area Low Voltage Products, Automation Technology Products Division Asia South, in the third plaintiff.

fact_changes.from: With effect from 1 January 2001, ABB Industry Pte Ltd became the employing entity for Sher Hock Guan Charles following a reorganisation of the ABB Singapore Group.
fact_changes.to: With effect from 1 January 2001, the third plaintiff became the employing entity for Sher Hock Guan Charles following a reorganisation of the ABB Singapore Group.

fact_changes.from: In February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles left the employment of ABB Industry Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.to: In February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles left the employment of the third plaintiff.

fact_changes.from: In late October 2002, while still employed by ABB Industry Pte Ltd, Sher Hock Guan Charles attended the 10th anniversary celebrations of ABB Xiamen Switchgear Co Ltd in Xiamen, China.
fact_changes.to: In late October 2002, while still employed by the third plaintiff, Sher Hock Guan Charles attended the 10th anniversary celebrations of ABB Xiamen Switchgear Co Ltd in Xiamen, China.

fact_changes.from: During the October 2002 ABB Xiamen anniversary event, representatives of Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute informed Sher Hock Guan Charles that they were looking for a technical adviser for research and development projects relating to a new generation of medium voltage switchgear, circuit breakers and vacuum interrupters.
fact_changes.to: During the October 2002 ABB Xiamen anniversary event, representatives of XIHARI informed Sher Hock Guan Charles that they were looking for a technical adviser for research and development projects relating to a new generation of medium voltage switchgear, circuit breakers and vacuum interrupters.

fact_changes.from: In November 2002, officers of Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute discussed by telephone with Sher Hock Guan Charles their wish to engage an internationally known technical adviser and mentioned the name of Guenther Leonhardt.
fact_changes.to: In November 2002, officers of XIHARI discussed by telephone with Sher Hock Guan Charles their wish to engage an internationally known technical adviser and mentioned the name of Mr Leonhardt.

fact_changes.from: Between 3 December 2002 and 3 February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles and Guenther Leonhardt exchanged nine e-mails concerning enquiries from Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute about engaging Leonhardt as a technical adviser for research and development projects on medium voltage switchgear, circuit breakers and vacuum interrupters and as a technical controller for a manufacturing company that would produce and market such products in China and other Asian markets.
fact_changes.to: Between 3 December 2002 and 3 February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles and Mr Leonhardt exchanged nine e-mails concerning enquiries from XIHARI about engaging Mr Leonhardt as a technical adviser for research and development projects on medium voltage switchgear, circuit breakers and vacuum interrupters and as a technical controller for a manufacturing company that would produce and market such products in China and other Asian markets.

fact_changes.from: In November 2002, an officer of Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute met Sher Hock Guan Charles in Singapore and asked him to check with Guenther Leonhardt on the possibility of engaging Leonhardt as a technical adviser.
fact_changes.to: In November 2002, an officer of XIHARI met Sher Hock Guan Charles in Singapore and asked him to check with Mr Leonhardt on the possibility of engaging Mr Leonhardt as a technical adviser.

fact_changes.from: By the end of January 2003, Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd had substantially completed development and critical type testing of a vacuum circuit breaker product, with testing having started on 25 October 2002 and completed on 13 March 2003.
fact_changes.to: By the end of January 2003, Huadian had finished prototyping and the most critical type testing; testing had begun on 25 October 2002 and was fully completed on 13 March 2003.

fact_changes.from: By the end of January 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles had decided to leave the employment of ABB Industry Pte Ltd and had informed a senior officer, Tom Sjoekvist, of this decision.
fact_changes.to: By the end of January 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles had decided to leave the employment of the third plaintiff and had informed a senior officer, Tom Sjoekvist, of this decision.

fact_changes.from: On 3 March 2003, after leaving ABB Industry Pte Ltd, Sher Hock Guan Charles commenced employment as General Manager of Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd.
fact_changes.to: On 3 March 2003, after leaving the third plaintiff, Sher Hock Guan Charles commenced employment as General Manager of Huadian.

fact_changes.from: By 28 March 2004, Gelpag mbH, a German company in which Sher Hock Guan Charles was a shareholder, had acquired 16.2% of the shares in Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd, alongside Fu Hsiang International Pte Ltd and Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute.
fact_changes.to: By 28 March 2004, Gelpag mbH, a German company in which Sher Hock Guan Charles was a shareholder, had acquired 16.2% of the shares in Huadian, alongside Fu Hsiang and XIHARI.

fact_changes.from: On or about 5 or 6 March 2003, shortly after joining Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd, Sher Hock Guan Charles telephoned Guenther Leonhardt and invited him to visit Huadian’s premises in Xiamen to discuss a potential technical consultancy role.
fact_changes.to: On or about 5 or 6 March 2003, shortly after joining Huadian, Sher Hock Guan Charles telephoned Mr Leonhardt and invited him to visit Huadian’s premises in Xiamen to discuss a potential technical consultancy role.

fact_changes.from: In late March 2003, a technical consultancy agreement between Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd and Guenther Leonhardt was signed and backdated to 1 March 2003.
fact_changes.to: In late March 2003, a technical consultancy agreement between Huadian and Mr Leonhardt was signed and backdated to 1 March 2003.

fact_changes.from: In July 2003, Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd commenced marketing its vacuum circuit breaker product and received its first order for that product in September 2003.
fact_changes.to: In July 2003, Huadian commenced marketing its vacuum circuit breaker product and received its first order for that product in September 2003.

fact_changes.from: On 18 February 2002, Great Vision International Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore by Sher Hock Sing and two other individuals, and this company was used to undertake a property development project in Xiamen through a subsidiary, Xiamen Fu Cheng Real Estate Property Development Co. Ltd.
fact_changes.to: On 18 February 2002, Great Vision International Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore by Sher Hock Sing and two other individuals, and this company was used to undertake a property development project in Xiamen through a subsidiary, Xiamen Fu Cheng.

fact_changes.from: In February 2002, Sher Hock Guan Charles became aware of an opportunity to develop a plot of land in Xiamen and passed this opportunity to his brother, Sher Hock Sing, and business associate Lin Qunxing, leading to the incorporation of Great Vision International Pte Ltd to pursue the project.
fact_changes.to: In February 2002, Sher Hock Guan Charles became aware of an opportunity to develop a plot of land in Xiamen and passed this opportunity to his brother, Sher Hock Sing, and business associate Mr Lin, leading to the incorporation of Great Vision International Pte Ltd to pursue the project.

fact_changes.from: On 3 December 2002, a payment voucher of Xiamen Fu Cheng Real Estate Property Development Co. Ltd recorded a cash advance of RMB70,000 described as a travel expenses loan, with the name of Sher Hock Guan Charles stated on the voucher and payment acknowledged as received by Huang Jianhuang.
fact_changes.to: On 3 December 2002, a payment voucher of Xiamen Fu Cheng recorded a cash advance of RMB70,000 described as a travel expenses loan, with the name of Sher Hock Guan Charles stated on the voucher and payment acknowledged as received by Huang Jianhuang.

fact_changes.from: On 25 April 2003, approximately two months after leaving ABB Industry Pte Ltd, Sher Hock Guan Charles was appointed a director of Great Vision International Pte Ltd and acquired 153 shares in that company from his brother, who resigned as director on the same date.
fact_changes.to: On 25 April 2003, approximately two months after leaving the third plaintiff, Sher Hock Guan Charles was appointed a director of Great Vision International Pte Ltd and acquired 153 shares in that company from his brother, who resigned as director on the same date.

fact_changes.from: On 25 April 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles became a director and a holder of one paid-up share in Webmoney Investment (S) Pte Ltd, acting as a Singapore-resident director to assist his associate Luo Baocheng.
fact_changes.to: On 25 April 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles became a director and a holder of one paid-up share in Webmoney Investment (S) Pte Ltd, acting as a Singapore-resident director to assist his friend Mr Luo.

fact_changes.from: By March 2004, Gelpag mbH funded its acquisition of a 16.2% shareholding in Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd using returns that Sher Hock Guan Charles had obtained from the Xiamen property development project undertaken through Great Vision International Pte Ltd and Xiamen Fu Cheng Real Estate Property Development Co. Ltd.
fact_changes.to: By March 2004, Gelpag mbH funded its acquisition of a 16.2% shareholding in Huadian using returns that Sher Hock Guan Charles had obtained from the Xiamen property development project undertaken through Great Vision and Xiamen Fu Cheng.

case_name: ABB Holdings Pte Ltd and Others v Sher Hock Guan Charles

fact_changes.from: From January 1997 to January 2000, Sher Hock Guan Charles held the position of President of ABB Installation Materials (East Asia) Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.to: From January 1997 to January 2000, Sher Hock Guan Charles held the position of President of the second plaintiff.

fact_changes.from: From January 2001 to February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles was General Manager of ABB Industry Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.to: From January 2001 to February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles was General Manager of the third plaintiff.

fact_changes.from: From September 2001 to February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles was Vice-President and Head of Business Area Low Voltage Products, Automation Technology Products Division Asia South, in ABB Industry Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.to: From September 2001 to February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles was Vice-President and Head of Business Area Low Voltage Products, Automation Technology Products Division Asia South, in the third plaintiff.

fact_changes.from: On 19 July 1996, Sher Hock Guan Charles signed an employment contract with ABB Holdings Pte Ltd covering the period from 1 January 1997 to 31 January 2000, which stated that he would serve as President of ABB Installation Materials (East Asia) Pte Ltd and use his best endeavours to promote the interest and welfare of the ABB Group.
fact_changes.to: On 19 July 1996, Sher Hock Guan Charles signed an employment contract with the first plaintiff covering the period from 1 January 1997 to 31 January 2000, which stated that he would serve as President of the second plaintiff and use his best endeavours to promote the interest and welfare of the ABB Group.

fact_changes.from: On 20 September 1999, Sher Hock Guan Charles signed a second employment contract, effective from 1 February 2000, which required him to maintain and preserve secrecy in all office matters, not to divulge information to outside members, and stated that private participation in other business activities, especially in the company’s line of business, was strictly not allowed.
fact_changes.to: On 20 September 1999, Sher Hock Guan Charles signed a second employment contract, effective from 1 February 2000, which required him to maintain and preserve secrecy in all office matters, not to divulge information to outside members, and stated that private participation in other business activities, especially in our line of business, was strictly not allowed.

fact_changes.from: On 15 January 2001, Sher Hock Guan Charles received and accepted a letter from ABB Industry Pte Ltd transferring his employment from ABB Installation Materials (East Asia) Pte Ltd to ABB Industry Pte Ltd with retrospective effect from 1 January 2001, attaching a Summary of Terms and Conditions of Employment for staff category M1/M2.
fact_changes.to: On 15 January 2001, Sher Hock Guan Charles received and accepted a letter from the third plaintiff transferring his employment from the second plaintiff to the third plaintiff with retrospective effect from 1 January 2001, attaching a Summary of Terms and Conditions of Employment for staff category M1/M2.

fact_changes.from: From 1 January 2001, Sher Hock Guan Charles’s employer of record became ABB Industry Pte Ltd, while he continued to hold a directorship in ABB Installation Materials (East Asia) Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.to: From 1 January 2001, Sher Hock Guan Charles’s employer of record became the third plaintiff, while he continued to hold a directorship in the second plaintiff.

fact_changes.from: In February 2002, after being informed by Xiamen Mayor Hong Yong Shi of a land development opportunity in Xiamen, Sher Hock Guan Charles relayed this opportunity to his brother, Sher Hock Sing, and business associate Lin Qunxing, leading to the incorporation of Great Vision International Pte Ltd on 18 February 2002 to pursue the project.
fact_changes.to: In 2002, Sher Hock Guan Charles relayed a land development opportunity in Xiamen to his brother, Sher Hock Sing, and business associate Lin Qunxing, leading to the incorporation of Great Vision International Pte Ltd on 18 February 2002 to pursue the project.

fact_changes.from: On 3 December 2002, a payment voucher of Xiamen Fu Cheng Real Estate Property Development Co. Ltd recorded a cash advance of RMB70,000 described as a travel expenses loan, listing Sher Hock Guan Charles as payee and applicant, with receipt acknowledged by Huang Jianhuang.
fact_changes.to: On 3 December 2002, a payment voucher of Xiamen Fu Cheng Real Estate Property Development Co. Ltd recorded a cash advance of RMB70,000 described as a travel expenses loan, with receipt acknowledged by Huang Jianhuang.

fact_changes.from: In late October 2002, while still employed by ABB Industry Pte Ltd, Sher Hock Guan Charles attended the 10th anniversary celebrations of ABB Xiamen Switchgear Co Ltd in Xiamen, China, where he met representatives of Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute.
fact_changes.to: In late October 2002, while still employed by the third plaintiff, Sher Hock Guan Charles attended the 10th anniversary celebrations of ABB Xiamen Switchgear Co Ltd in Xiamen, China, where he met representatives of Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute.

fact_changes.from: During the ABB Xiamen anniversary event in October 2002, officers of Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute informed Sher Hock Guan Charles that they were looking for a technical adviser for research and development projects relating to a new generation of medium voltage switchgear, circuit breakers and vacuum interrupters.
fact_changes.to: During the ABB Xiamen anniversary event in October 2002, officers of Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute informed Sher Hock Guan Charles that they were looking for a technical adviser for research and development projects.

fact_changes.from: On 4 December 2002, Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd was incorporated in China as a joint venture company with founding shareholders Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute and Fu Hsiang International Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.to: In December 2002, Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd was incorporated in China as a joint venture company with founding shareholders XIHARI and Fu Hsiang International Pte Ltd.

fact_changes.from: By the end of January 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles had decided to leave ABB Industry Pte Ltd and had informed senior ABB officer Tom Sjoekvist of his intention to resign.
fact_changes.to: By the end of January 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles had decided to leave the third plaintiff and had informed senior ABB officer Tom Sjoekvist of his intention to resign.

fact_changes.from: By 31 January 2003, Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd had completed prototyping and critical type testing of a vacuum circuit breaker product, with testing having commenced on 25 October 2002.
fact_changes.to: By 31 January 2003, Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd had finished prototyping and the most critical type testing, with testing having commenced on 25 October 2002.

fact_changes.from: On 3 March 2003, after leaving ABB Industry Pte Ltd, Sher Hock Guan Charles commenced employment as General Manager of Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd.
fact_changes.to: On 3 March 2003, after leaving the third plaintiff, Sher Hock Guan Charles commenced employment as General Manager of Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd.

fact_changes.from: On 25 April 2003, approximately two months after leaving ABB Industry Pte Ltd, Sher Hock Guan Charles was appointed a director of Great Vision International Pte Ltd and acquired 153 shares in that company from his brother, who resigned as director on the same date.
fact_changes.to: On 25 April 2003, approximately two months after leaving the third plaintiff, Sher Hock Guan Charles was appointed a director of Great Vision International Pte Ltd and acquired 153 shares in that company from his brother, who resigned as director on the same date.

fact_changes.from: In May 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles incorporated Gelpag International Pte Ltd in Singapore and became a director and shareholder of this company.
fact_changes.to: In May 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles procured the incorporation of Gelpag International Pte Ltd in Singapore and became a director and shareholder of this company.

fact_changes.from: By March 2004, Gelpag mbH acquired a 16.2% shareholding in Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd at par value, funded using profits that Sher Hock Guan Charles had obtained from the Xiamen property development project undertaken through Great Vision International Pte Ltd and Xiamen Fu Cheng Real Estate Property Development Co. Ltd.
fact_changes.to: By March 2004, Gelpag mbH acquired a 16.2% shareholding in Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd at par value, funded using profits that Sher Hock Guan Charles had obtained from the Xiamen property development project.
